title: Methuen High School teacher on leave amid allegations of ‘inappropriate conduct’
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News Police opened a criminal investigation after school officials reported the teacher “was potentially engaged in inappropriate conduct and communications with a student.
” Methuen High School.

Jim Davis/Boston Globe Staff, File By Abby Patkin April 16, 2026 | 1:31 PM 1 minute to read A Methuen High School teacher is on paid administrative leave and facing a criminal investigation amid allegations of “inappropriate conduct” with a student, police and school officials said.

title: Vandals target nonprofit farm in Ipswich
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News Thousands of dollars worth of seedlings were destroyed at the Three Sisters greenhouse, dampening the start of the farming season.
A photo of the greenhouse before it was vandalized at Three Sisters.

Courtesy of Three Sisters Garden Project Earlier this week, members of the nonprofit Three Sisters Garden Project walked into their greenhouse on Washington Street in Ipswich to find that someone had destroyed more than half of their seedlings, causing thousands of dollars in damage.

title: New records show how police tracked down the alleged Beverly mansion robber
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Crime The suspect's ex-girlfriend worked as a maid at the historic Beverly mansion, police found.
They are still searching for a second, unidentified suspect.
This historic mansion on Paine Avenue in Beverly was robbed by masked thieves.
